# Agent Hand - Complete Documentation

> A fast, Rust-based terminal session manager (TUI) for AI coding agents like Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and OpenCode. Built on tmux with <50ms startup and ~8MB memory usage.

---

## Overview

Agent Hand is a terminal user interface (TUI) application written in Rust that manages multiple AI coding agent sessions. When developers run 5+ AI agents simultaneously (Claude Code, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Copilot), they lose track of which sessions need input, which are running, and which are idle. Agent Hand solves this by providing a unified dashboard with real-time status detection, instant session switching, and smart prioritization.

It runs on a dedicated tmux server (`agent-hand-server`) so it never conflicts with the user's existing tmux configuration or sessions.

## Technical Architecture

- **Language**: Rust (2021 edition)
- **TUI Framework**: ratatui + crossterm
- **Terminal Backend**: tmux (dedicated server instance)
- **Binary Size**: ~2.7MB (release build)
- **Startup Time**: <50ms
- **Memory Usage**: ~8MB typical
- **Platforms**: macOS (Intel + Apple Silicon), Linux (x86_64, aarch64), Windows via WSL

### How It Works

1. Agent Hand spawns or attaches to a dedicated tmux server (`agent-hand-server`)
2. Each AI agent session runs in its own tmux window
3. The TUI monitors each session's PTY output to detect status (Waiting for input, Running, Idle)
4. Users navigate sessions via keyboard shortcuts, fuzzy search, or priority jump
5. The dashboard renders real-time status, session groups, labels, and PTY metrics

## Installation

### One-liner (recommended)

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/weykon/agent-hand/master/install.sh | bash
```

This downloads the latest release binary for your platform and installs it to `~/.local/bin/agent-hand`.

### Build from Source

Requires Rust toolchain (rustup):

```bash
git clone https://github.com/weykon/agent-hand.git
cd agent-hand
cargo build --release
# Binary at target/release/agent-hand
```

### Dependencies

- tmux >= 3.0 (required)
- A terminal emulator with 256-color support (recommended)

## Core Features (Free)

### Status Detection
Agent Hand monitors each session's terminal output and classifies the status:
- **WAITING** (yellow !): Session is waiting for user input (e.g., Claude Code asking a question)
- **RUNNING** (blue spinner): Agent is actively working
- **READY** (green checkmark): Task completed successfully
- **IDLE** (gray circle): No activity

### Priority Jump (Ctrl+N)
Instantly jumps to the most urgent session — typically one in WAITING status that needs user input. This prevents the common problem of missing a Claude Code confirmation prompt buried in one of many terminal tabs.

### Fuzzy Switcher (Ctrl+G)
Opens an overlay search that matches against session names, group names, and labels. Type a few characters to filter and jump directly to any session.

### Session Groups & Labels
Organize sessions by project or purpose:
- Groups: `/work`, `/debug`, `/experiments`
- Labels: Custom colored labels for visual identification
- Create via `n` key, manage groups via the session creation dialog

### PTY Monitoring
Real-time tracking of pseudo-terminal resource usage across all sessions, with a system-wide gauge bar and warnings when approaching limits.

### tmux Isolation
Agent Hand uses its own tmux server (`agent-hand-server`), completely separate from your default tmux. Your existing tmux config, sessions, and keybindings are untouched.

## Keyboard Shortcuts

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| `j` / `Down` | Navigate down in session list |
| `k` / `Up` | Navigate up in session list |
| `Enter` | Attach to selected session |
| `n` | Create new session |
| `d` | Delete session |
| `r` | Rename session |
| `Ctrl+N` | Priority jump (next urgent session) |
| `Ctrl+G` | Fuzzy switcher |
| `q` | Quit |
| `/` | Search sessions |
| `g` | Manage groups |
| `?` | Show help |

## Pro Features ($19 one-time)

### Auto-Upgrade Command
Run `agent-hand upgrade` to automatically download and install the latest version. No manual download needed.

### Priority Support
Direct support channel for Pro license holders.

### Future Premium Features
Pro license holders get access to all future premium features as they're released.

## Max Features ($9/month or $89/year)

### AI Session Summarizer
AI-powered summaries of terminal session activity. Understand what each AI agent accomplished without reading through the full terminal output.

### Remote Sharing & Collaboration
Share terminal sessions with team members in real-time:
- **WebSocket Relay**: Primary sharing method via encrypted WebSocket connection
- **tmate Fallback**: SSH-based sharing as backup
- **Viewer Roles**: Read-only viewers and read-write collaborators
- **Control Request Flow**: Viewers can request control, hosts approve/deny

### Session Relationships & Context
Link related AI agent sessions together:
- Parent-child relationships between sessions
- Context sharing across related sessions
- Visual relationship indicators in the dashboard

## Pricing Details

| Tier | Price | Payment | Features |
|------|-------|---------|----------|
| Free | $0 | - | Core TUI, tmux integration, status detection, groups, fuzzy search |
| Pro | $19 | One-time | Everything in Free + auto-upgrade, priority support |
| Max | $9/mo or $89/yr | Subscription | Everything in Pro + AI summarizer, remote sharing, session relationships |

After purchase, activate with:
```bash
agent-hand login
```

## Comparison with Alternatives

### vs. raw tmux
tmux provides terminal multiplexing but no AI-agent-specific features. Agent Hand adds status detection, priority jumping, fuzzy search, session grouping, and a visual dashboard on top of tmux.

### vs. agent-deck (Go)
Agent Hand is a Rust rewrite of the original agent-deck concept. It offers better performance (<50ms startup vs ~200ms), lower memory usage (~8MB vs ~30MB), and additional features like fuzzy search, PTY monitoring, and remote collaboration.

### vs. screen/byobu
These are general-purpose terminal multiplexers without AI agent management capabilities. Agent Hand is purpose-built for managing AI coding agent sessions.

## Use Cases

### Solo Developer with Multiple AI Agents
Running Claude Code on 3 different features + Gemini CLI for research + OpenCode for testing. Agent Hand shows which ones need input at a glance.

### Team Collaboration
Share a debugging session with a colleague via remote sharing. They can watch in real-time or request control to help.

### CI/CD Monitoring
Use Agent Hand to monitor multiple long-running AI-assisted build/test sessions simultaneously.

## System Requirements

- **OS**: macOS 12+, Linux (kernel 5.0+), Windows 10+ (via WSL2)
- **tmux**: 3.0 or later
- **Terminal**: Any terminal with 256-color support
- **Disk**: ~3MB for the binary
- **RAM**: ~8MB during operation
